meaning: adj.现存的,未毁的,<古>突出的,显著的
examples: The oldest extant document is dated 1492.
The body of extant scholarship on Griffith was so tiny that it could all be read in a day.
A medieval manuscript called L contains all eighteen extant tragedies by the Greek playwright Euripides.
Macaya is the largest extant biologically intact series of connected ecosystems in the country.
The program goes beyond limited test-drive or quick-return programs extant at other brands, Perry said.
